message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
checking
Uh oh...here we go again
@sakib.malik-zomato.com can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
Right now, I'm seeing about 40% of payment transactions failing during checkout, with a noticeable spike in customer complaints.
gettting this error: Payment Unavailable
:star: Reproduces the incident
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
@bob can you check, users are getting issues while checking out
I will start checking the logs now
Could it be your checkout change again @Shay? It broke the platform last week!
The checkout service logs are healthy and the service is working as intended
*Incident Number INC100066:* SEV 2
started incident
I see 504 HTTP errors in the payment service, which means the application is failing to serve the requests
I need specifics. How many users are impacted? Which regions are affected? Are we currently losing revenue?
I'm observing that the payment service health check is also returning 504s and latency has increased sharply.
A 504 error typically means the payment service is timing out while waiting for a response from an upstream dependency.
A 504 Gateway Timeout indicates the payment service is unable to reach its upstream dependency or is taking too long to respond.
Despite normal CPU and memory usage, the payment service's latency has spiked significantly since the incident began.
recent changes done by @tanya @daniel please verify
:star: Check changes done to the platform
:star: Reviewed changes
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
Noted on the incident number. I still need details: what is the scale of customer impact, and which regions are experiencing this? Please provide numbers and locations.
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
Any updates on the investigation?
:star: Qualify the problem
when did the incident start @bob
The first spike in payment failures appeared about 10 minutes ago, with a 100% failure rate since then.
can you qualify how many users are getting affected across uk ?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Currently, 100% of payment attempts from UK users are failing, with zero successful transactions in the last 10 minutes.
@shay @daniel can you check this change: CHG89360
I've checked the logs around the time of CHG89360 and noticed that 504 errors from the payment service started immediately after this deployment.
Currently, 100% of payment attempts from UK users are failing, with zero successful transactions in the last 10 minutes.

Given the timing, it's likely that something in CHG89360 is causing the payment service to time out for UK transactions, so we should review the changes for any region-specific logic or network calls.
Understood. This is a critical revenue impact for the UK. What is the estimated time to resolution? Keep me updated on any change in transaction success rates.
can we check if this is safe to revert this deploy
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Yes, it's safe to rollback CHG89360 as there are no new dependencies or schema changes introduced in this deploy.
cool, @shay can you start revert, we can debug later in rca
I will rollback my checkout change right away
sure, we are starting revert
Rollback complete!
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
rollback completed
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
Something else must be causing this
We need this fixed!
yes, still seeing failures
Metrics confirm there has been no improvement in UK payment success rates since the rollback.
Since Shay's rollback didn't help, maybe we should look at other recent changes, but my code was thoroughly tested and shouldn't be the cause.
@daniel can you check what is this change: CHG89361
CHG89361 is my recent update to the Bitcoin payment service, mainly refactoring and some enhancements, but all unit tests passed and it shouldn't affect other flows.
Has payment flow been restored? Are UK transactions processing successfully now and is revenue back to normal levels?
can we try reverting if this is safe to revert ?
The new code isn't being invoked yet, can I investigate a bit more.
still failing, we are checking
yes, please check if there are any errors
I'll check the logs for any errors related to my change and report back shortly.
@bob is this happening for any particular payment method ?
The failures are only affecting GBP payments, while other currencies seem unaffected.
any updates @daniel ?
Understood. I need a clear ETA for resolution. Continue to update me on progress and any changes in transaction success. This outage is unacceptable—prioritize restoring revenue flow.
:star: Manage actions and timings
I'm still reviewing the logs, but so far I haven't found any obvious errors related to my change.
sure
can you also check this change: CHG89362
CHG89362 was a deployment to the PaymentDatabase service to improve indexing and transaction processing, and I haven't seen any errors or alerts from that rollout.
@daniel can you see errors in currency service ?
I haven't seen any errors in the currency service logs so far, but I'll keep digging to be sure.
Waiting for your next update. I expect immediate notification as soon as there is any progress or change in impact.
@tanya what was this change related to: CHG89350
CHG89350 was a PaymentDatabase deployment aimed at improving indexing and transaction processing, and there have been no alerts or errors reported from it so far.
sure, will notify
If the issue started 10 mins ago, and my change has been rolled back, then the other suspicious change is in the payments made by Daniel around 15 mins back
And all the complaints are around payments failure
frontend errors have increased @daniel
can you check once
If frontend errors are up, maybe something in the shared libraries I touched is surfacing there, but I still don't see how my Bitcoin changes would impact GBP payments.
I dug into Daniel's changes and found he modified some shared libraries that are used in the GBP payment flows, which could be affecting UK transactions.
Acknowledged. I expect prompt updates—especially when you have a fix or a revised ETA. This remains top priority.
We should be looking for rolling back CHG89362
@daniel safe to revert this change ?
I really think my code is solid and not being called in these flows, can we check more logs or metrics before rolling back?
next update in 5 mins
:star: Track actions and time box them
@daniel lets prioritize resolution for now, we can debug later
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now
thanks
Rollback complete. Might take a few minutes to stabilise
Did that fix it?
:bulb: To complete this drill, ask the engineering team to verify the payment service is working, and check with Bob if UK customer payments have recovered.
Would you like me to try placing a test order to confirm if payments are working again?
Understood. I’ll expect your update in 5 minutes. Make sure to include transaction status and revenue impact in your next message.
checking
Do you want me to run a verification transaction to help confirm if the rollback resolved the issue?
able to place order now
:star: Validate the fix
Great to hear orders are going through again—let me know if you need any more checks from my side.
seems to be fixed, error rate on fe is also down
:star: Use metrics as evidence
That's great news—let me know if you need any further verification or support from platform.
@bob can you check if customers are still facing the issue
Looking into it now... Metrics seem to be improving!
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally
what was the impact
From what we can see, UK customers were unable to complete payments for about 30 minutes. No other regions were affected, and the issue appears to be resolved now.
issue is now resolved after we reverted the buggy change, uk costomers were not able to place order for 30 mins
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
it is now fixed
I don't think the Beatles would've anticipated cryptocurrency so let's keep it that way for now
Confirm that UK payments are processing successfully and revenue is flowing as expected. I want assurance that all impacted customers can now complete orders without issue.

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

yes, error rate is down, able to place order confirmed from business team

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
https://learn.uptimelabs.io/scenario/online-boutique/levels/5/8254/feedback
Good. Prepare a summary of total orders and revenue lost during the 30-minute outage. I want this on my desk within the hour.
